news.title: India gets tough on international branch campuses
news.summary_en: New research and sector discussions suggest India’s international branch campus ecosystem is entering a more demanding era, with pressure mounting on outcomes, employability and long-term relevance.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: MPs and university leaders crowned winners of 2026 Duolingo Language Challenge
news.summary_en: Alex Mayer and Vanessa Lemm have been named the top winners of the 2026 Duolingo Language Challenge, after competing against more than 1,300 parliamentarians and university staff across the UK.
news.author: Beth Kennedy

news.title: UK-EU youth mobility talks stall despite warming ties
news.summary_en: While the EU ambassador to the UK praised the strengthening UK-EU relationship at a recent industry event, reports suggest disagreements over the anticipated Youth Experience Scheme are hampering the reset.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Korea’s international students strapped with low-paid jobs
news.summary_en: New report finds 71% of working international students are employed in food and accommodation jobs, a week after Seoul unveiled a “quality-focused” rethink of international student policy and post-study pathways
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: UK study visa applications plunge 40% in April
news.summary_en: UK study visa applications this year have dropped by a third compared to 2025, as new Home Office data reveals the fewest April applicants in five years.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: OfS: English universities warned of “over-optimism” as a third face deficits
news.summary_en: England’s higher education watchdog has revealed over a third of universities faced deficits last year, with the figure set to rise in 2025/26.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: King’s College London and Cranfield announce proposed 2027 merger
news.summary_en: King’s College London and Cranfield University have announced plans to merge in 2027, with university leaders saying the move would create a globally competitive institution spanning engineering, defence, environmental science and business education.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: New bipartisan caucus backs US study abroad
news.summary_en: A bipartisan group of US policymakers has launched a caucus to bolster support for international exchange programs in Congress.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: UK universities launch China ‘High Achievers Program’ to boost student pathways
news.summary_en: A group of UK universities has launched a new initiative aimed at strengthening pathways for Chinese students, as institutions look to move beyond competition and towards collaboration in a challenging recruitment market.
news.author: Amy Baker

news.title: NZ international enrolments hit 92k as recovery gathers pace
news.summary_en: New Zealand’s international education recovery is accelerating, with enrolments climbing 11% year-on-year to reach 92,580 students in 2025, according to new figures from Education New Zealand (ENZ).
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: ICE director brands OPT a “magnet for fraud”
news.summary_en: The head of America’s notorious Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has called the US post-study work program a “magnet for fraud”, vowing to “relentlessly investigate” students and employers.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Revealed: the best-performing agents for New Zealand
news.summary_en: Immigration New Zealand has published the top-performing agents for nine source countries based on application volume and visa approval rates. The data also reveals a higher visa approval rate for agent-linked applications than in previous years.
news.author: The PIE News team
